title: Renaissance Scholarship — Gratitude Letter Draft (Unfinished)
html:
TL;DR

  • An unfinished, rambling first-draft thank-you letter to the Renaissance organization — a third scholarship body alongside Ford Family Foundation and JCEF in the November 2020 application sprint.
  • Opens with formal grace: "I want to begin by extending my gratitude for the opportunity the Renaissance organization has presented me. It is an unbelievable feeling to know that people believe in my potential."
  • Then derails mid-sentence twice — "Like many, I had an all in scholarship During my scholarshipI underWhen I was applying for the scholarship, I put my heart into my essays..." — Yahir mid-thought, fragments overlapping. Captures the cognitive-load reality of being a 19-year-old writing simultaneous thank-you letters to multiple funders.
  • The note's value is the psychological signal: the debt of gratitude pattern operating in real time — gratitude felt sincerely, but the medium (formal letter) doesn't fit Yahir's voice yet, so the draft fragments.
  • Captured July 6, 2021 — same day as the Diana Hoff summer to do sweep; pre-OSU admin-density window.

Key quotes

"It is an unbelievable feeling to know that people believe in my potential."

"Like many, I had an all in scholarship During my scholarshipI underWhen I was applying for the scholarship, I put my heart into my essays"

Open questions

  • Did the final Renaissance letter get sent? No follow-up note confirms — possibly drafted in a different Apple Note or sent as a Google Doc.
  • Renaissance — single explicit mention so far; organization name candidate. Add to potential entities.

Connections

debt of gratitude · ford family foundation · jcef · osu · 2020 11 15 scholarship essays todo · 2021 07 06 contact diana hoff summer todos
